purpose: Defines the differential write protocol between !p (lightweight) and !p_track (full tracking) execution modes — which files the master writes, when, and at what granularity. Also specifies auto-escalation thresholds for automatic parallel mode and the master-written progress file bridge for lightweight/serial dispatches. Loaded whenever the master must decide HOW to populate a dashboard for a given execution mode.
gotchas: The >1 wave threshold is ABSOLUTELY non-negotiable — multi-wave swarms ALWAYS get full !p_track tracking regardless of agent count. Even 2-task/2-wave swarms must use full tracking.
The 'ide' dashboard is RESERVED — master agents must NEVER write swarm initialization, logs, or progress to it. It is permanent and auto-created on Electron startup.
In !p mode, workers do NOT write progress files — the master MUST bridge by writing minimal progress files from worker returns, otherwise the dashboard has no file change tracking or per-task detail.
If the assigned dashboard contains previous data, the master MUST ASK the user before archiving — do not proceed without approval (NON-NEGOTIABLE assigned-dashboard rule).

Worker prompts use TEMPLATE_VERSION 'p_track_v2' for full tracking and 'p_v2' for lightweight — using the wrong version causes workers to either skip progress files (full mode) or attempt writes that aren't expected (lite mode).
logs.json is ALWAYS written, even in !p mode — at minimum the init entry, per-task completion entries, and the swarm complete summary.
Always clear progress/ before writing initialization.json — stale progress from a previous swarm will appear on cards if not cleared.
patterns: !p_track writes 5 files: initialization.json (write-once), logs.json (continuous), progress/{id}.json (workers), master_state.json (after every event), metrics.json (write-once at end).
!p writes 2 files: initialization.json (write-once) + logs.json (init entry + final batch). NO master_state.json or metrics.json.
Auto-escalation: if 3+ parallel agents OR >1 wave -> full !p_track tracking; otherwise lightweight.
Both modes follow the same dashboard-selection priority chain: assigned dashboard (NON-NEGOTIABLE) -> --dashboard flag -> ask user.
Master-written progress file schema in lightweight mode: status, started_at, completed_at, summary, assigned_agent, stage, message, milestones[], deviations[], logs[], files_changed[].
Parse worker FILES CHANGED return into files_changed[]: {created|modified|deleted} prefix becomes the action field.
conventions: Archive before clear: copy entire dashboard dir to {tracker_root}/Archive/{YYYY-MM-DD}_{task_name}/ before resetting state — applies in BOTH modes, no exceptions.
logs.json !p entries: only init, per-task completion, per-task failure, and swarm-complete summary entries are written.
initialization.json schema is identical across both modes — dashboard renders the same plan view regardless of execution mode.
Failed worker progress files written by master use status='failed', stage='failed', and extract ERRORS section into a 'level: error' log entry.
